narration_text: Now we'll see how throughput works in a network scenario, let's think about this together. We have ten connections that fairly share a backbone bottleneck link with a rate of R bits per second. Look at the diagram on your slide, notice how the links and nodes are arranged, and observe the bottleneck link in the middle. You should see that all the connections are sharing this one link, which means they're all competing for the same bandwidth. This is similar to a highway with multiple lanes merging into one, where the traffic has to slow down to accommodate all the cars. I want you to focus on how this bottleneck link affects the overall throughput of the network. As we move to the next slide, consider how this shared link impacts the performance of each individual connection.
